_README: The right answers, confirmed by Maoz on 2026-07-13. Without this file no accuracy
claim about the extractor means anything.
RULE for what counts as a cutout:
- enclosed holes (circle / rectangle / obround slot)  -> YES
- notches cut INTO the part, open to the edge         -> YES
- the outer profile itself, chamfers, gear teeth      -> NO  (that is the part's shape)
Sizes are REAL-WORLD mm (after applying the sheet scale), never paper mm.
scale = real_mm / paper_mm. A 1:5 sheet has scale 5.0; a 2:1 magnified sheet has 0.5.

cutouts.source: labelled 340 x 100, open to bottom edge

117-626-141_4_Rev.3_BLANK.pdf.note: title block says SCALE:1:1 and is WRONG - do not trust it

cutouts.source: unlabelled; measured from geometry, confirmed by Maoz

12562-3000F501023_03.pdf.note: MAGNIFIED sheet - paper is twice real size

333-532-294_2_BLANK.pdf.note: title block says SCALE:1:5 and is WRONG
must_not_detect: the Ø325 outer ring (that is the part)

ASH-071222-TW550-M10_BLANK.pdf.note: title block says SCALE:1:5 and is WRONG
must_not_detect: the Ø glyph in the text 'Ø290 THRU' - currently auto-approved as a Ø3.1 hole
the Ø551 toothed outer profile (that is the part)

A (4).pdf.note: no Scale text; inferred from the Ø10 label matching the 9.99mm measured holes

A (3).pdf.note: UNRESOLVED. No Scale text and no Ø label. Sheet carries 7128/3397/2857/300/216 mm labels, so it is certainly not 1:1. Needs the dimension-line-length method. Counts are known; sizes are not.

Doc_HK3573_290626083217_00 (1).pdf.note: UNRESOLVED scale. Earlier work recorded 18 holes + 5 slots as the expected counts; not re-confirmed against the drawing.
